name: adaptive_ns_steps
label: stage3_original
why: Schedule Newton-Schulz iterations: 7 early (noisy gradients need better ortho), 5 mid, 3 late (weights converging, cheaper is fine). From control theory gain scheduling: adapt controller precision to the operating regime. Nobody has tried varying NS steps over training.
validates: Adaptive NS improves throughput (fewer matmuls late) without quality loss, or improves quality (better ortho early) without throughput cost.
falsifies: 5 steps is universally optimal. NS convergence is fast enough that extra early steps don't help, and 3 late steps lose quality.
notes: Two potential wins: quality (early) and throughput (late).
At 180s screen: early phase is ~36s, enough to see the 7-step effect.
